mapping.a_element: extreme points of the convex set of density matrices with positive partial transpose (PPT)
mapping.b_element: lowest rank non-separable PPT states (rank 4 in 3x3 systems)

mapping.a_element: necessary and sufficient condition for extremality in PPT set
mapping.b_element: characterization of rank-4 PPT states as projections onto the orthogonal complement of a subspace spanned by an orthogonal Unextendible Product Basis (UPB)

mapping.a_element: algorithm for finding extreme points of PPT set
mapping.b_element: local equivalence transformation to orthogonal UPB form for rank-4 PPT states

brief_justification: While both papers study the structure of positive partial transpose (PPT) states, Paper A focuses on extremality conditions and convex geometry of the PPT set, whereas Paper B relies on algebraic geometry (Bezout's Theorem) and unextendible product bases to characterize specific low-rank PPT states. The underlying machinery—convex analysis vs. algebraic geometry—differs fundamentally.
